← index Overview Request flow HTTP API Storage

crate-html architecture

Five components, two binaries, one Go module, no database. This page shows the system from four angles: who-talks-to-whom, what's-in-the-code, what's-on-disk, and what-could-swap-in-later.

1. System view

Two binaries (crate, crated) and three outside actors (agent, browser, filesystem). Solid arrows are HTTP. Dashed are process or filesystem calls.

agent (external) crate CLI crated daemon browser filesystem
EdgeProtocolAuth
agent → crateshell execOS user
crate → crated (mgmt)HTTP /api/*Bearer token (32 random bytes)
browser → crated (read)HTTP /<site>/...none — bound to 127.0.0.1
crate → fsfile readfile mode 0600 on config
crated → fsfile read/writeOS user

2. Code view — Go module structure

One module, two binaries, six internal packages. internal/wire is the seam: both binaries import it, neither imports each other.

PackageResponsibilityDepends on
cmd/crateCLI entrypoint, kong wiring, command handlerscliclient, config
cmd/cratedDaemon entrypoint, http.Server lifecycle, signalsserver, storage, config
internal/wireHTTP request/response types — the contractnone
internal/configXDG path resolution, config load, first-run tokenadrg/xdg, yaml.v3
internal/storageSites on disk, tar in/out, atomic replacement, name validationwire
internal/serverHTTP handlers (API + static), bearer authstorage, wire, config
internal/cliclientHTTP client; tars a local dir into a streaming PUTstorage (for tar), wire, config
Why is cliclient allowed to import storage? Only to reuse WriteDirAsTar — the tar-writing helper is symmetric with the tar-reading helper on the server side, and lives in one place. Everything else in storage is filesystem-side; the CLI doesn't touch it.

3. Deployment view — what lives where

Everything is on the laptop. v0 has no remote components.

~/GitHub/crate-html/          # source
  bin/
    crate                  # the CLI binary
    crated                 # the daemon binary

~/Library/Application Support/crate/   # macOS XDG-equivalent
  config.yaml            # 0600 — port, base URL, token
  sites/
    welcome/
    overview/
    cratesplainer/
    architecture/         # ← you are here
  log/                   # (reserved for future)

On Linux these paths follow the XDG spec: config in ~/.config/crate/, data in ~/.local/share/crate/, state in ~/.local/state/crate/.

4. Layer cake — what could swap in later

The v0 design is deliberately thin so each future capability slots in as a layer without rewriting what's underneath.

Now · storage — filesystem under XDG_DATA_HOME
Now · daemon — crated binding 127.0.0.1:7777
Now · CLI + HTTP API — crate over bearer-authed HTTP
Now · static serve — http.ServeFile path-routed by site name
Now · agent skill — Claude Code skill at .claude/skills/crate-push/
Later · daemon lifecycle — launchd plist so crated survives terminal sessions
Later · Pi skill — same shape as Claude Code skill, different agent
Later · TLS / hostname — Caddy in front of crated, no code change
Later · tailnet exposure — reach the daemon from any tailnet device
Later · per-site TTLs — crate push --expires 24h for ephemeral drafts

Layers above the dashed line are running today; the dashed ones are roadmap items. None of them require touching the contract in internal/wire.

crate-html · overview · cratesplainer · all sites
